extraction.one_line_summary: SOLER Marc attacked on the descent after Alto San Miguel with 1km remaining, soloing to victory while Roglic took 6-second time bonus for second to extend GC lead.
tactical_patterns: Movistar's Verona set tempo early on final climb before Soler attacked during single hesitation moment on descent.
Roglic prioritized time bonuses over stage win, sprinting for second place to gain 6 seconds on Carapaz who scored nothing.
Dan Martin positioned on Roglic's wheel specifically to stay with fastest rider for time gains, moving to second overall.
Wellens attempted long-range solo from 40+ miles out on shallow Cat 3 climb suited to diesel power riders.

rider_signals.SOLER Marc: positive: Movistar home roads advantage materialized with decisive descending attack after hesitation, moving 20th to 10th overall in one stage
rider_signals.ROGLIC Primoz: positive: Tactically astute to prioritize time bonus sprint over chasing stage winner, extending GC lead through calculated restraint
rider_signals.MARTIN Dan: positive: Wheel selection intelligence riding Roglic specifically to maximize time gains, moved to second overall
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: negative: Failed to contest time bonus sprint at finish, losing 6 additional seconds to Roglic beyond stage time gaps
rider_signals.GROSSCHARTNER Felix: negative: Lost significant time after strong fifth place on Stage 1, dropped on final climb despite yesterday's strength
